curriculum.subject: Biology
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 26 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: The Living World, Biological Classification, Plant Kingdom, Animal Kingdom
topics: Biodiversity and Need for classification
Three domains of life, taxonomy and systematics
Concept of species and taxonomical hierarchy
Binomial nomenclature
Five kingdom classification system
Salient features of Monera, Protista and Fungi
Classification into major groups: Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
Classification of plants into major groups: Algae, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnospermae and Angiosperms
Classification of non-chordates up to phyla level and chordates upto class level

units.unitName: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
units.description: Morphology of Flowering Plants, Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Structural Organisation in Animals
topics: Morphology of root, stem, leaf, inflorescence, flower, fruit and seed
Tissue systems in dicots and monocots
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of digestive system

units.description: Cell-The Unit of Life, Biomolecules, Cell Cycle and Cell Division
topics: Cell theory and cell as basic unit
Chemical constituents: Proteins, Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids
Cell cycle phases

units.description: Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Respiration in Plants, Plant - Growth and Development
topics: Site of photosynthesis and pigments
Exchange of gases and cellular respiration pathways
Seed germination, phases of growth and rate

units.description: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Human Reproduction, Reproductive Health
topics: Flower structure and development of gametophytes
Pollination types, agencies, out-breeding devices, pollen-pistil interaction
Double fertilization and post-fertilization events (endosperm/embryo)
Development of seed and fruit formation
Special modes: apomixis, parthenocarpy, polyembryony
Male and female reproductive systems; microscopic anatomy of testis and ovary
Gametogenesis: spermatogenesis and oogenesis
Menstrual cycle, fertilization, embryo development to blastocyst
Implantation, pregnancy, placenta formation (elementary)
Parturition and lactation (elementary)
Need for reproductive health and prevention of STDs
Birth control: need, methods, contraception
Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP) and amniocentesis
Infertility and Assisted Reproductive Technologies (IVF, ZIFT, GIFT)
General awareness on reproductive health issues

units.description: Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Evolution
topics: Mendelian inheritance and deviations (incomplete dominance, co-dominance)
Multiple alleles, blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance
Chromosome theory of inheritance; sex determination mechanisms
Linkage, crossing over, sex-linked inheritance (haemophilia, colour blindness)
Mendelian and chromosomal disorders in humans
Search for genetic material; DNA and RNA structure
DNA packaging, replication, Central Dogma (transcription/translation)
Genetic code and gene expression regulation (lac operon)
Human genome project and rice genome projects
DNA fingerprinting
Origin of life and evidences for evolution (paleontology, anatomy, embryology)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory
Mechanism of evolution: variation via mutation and recombination

units.description: Human Health and Diseases, Microbes in Human Welfare
Biotechnology - Principles and Processes, Biotechnology and its Applications
Organisms and Populations, Ecosystem, Biodiversity and its Conservation

practicals.title: Study of locally available common flowering plants (Solanaceae)
practicals.unit_name: Unit-I Diversity of Living Organisms
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-1: The Living World / Chapter-2: Biological Classification
practicals.details: Dissection and display of floral whorls, anther, ovary; show number of chambers (floral formulae/diagrams), type of root, stem, leaf arrangement.

practicals.title: Preparation and study of T.S. of dicot and monocot roots and stems
practicals.unit_name: Unit-II Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-5: Morphology of Flowering Plants / Chapter-6: Anatomy of Flowering Plants
practicals.details: Primary transverse sections to study tissue systems.

practicals.title: Study of osmosis by potato osmometer
practicals.unit_name: Unit-V Human Physiology (Contextual)
practicals.details: Experiment demonstrating endosmosis in raisins or using a potato.

practicals.title: Study of plasmolysis in epidermal peels
practicals.unit_name: Unit-III Cell: Structure and Function
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-8: Cell-The Unit of Life / Chapter-9: Biomolecules
practicals.details: Using Rhoeo/lily leaves or onion bulb scale leaves.

practicals.title: Study of distribution of stomata on upper and lower surfaces of leaves
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Plant Physiology / Unit-V Human Physiology (Contextual)

practicals.title: Test for presence of sugar, starch, proteins and fats
practicals.unit_name: Unit-III Cell: Structure and Function / Unit-V Human Physiology (Contextual)
practicals.details: Testing suitable plant and animal materials.

practicals.title: Separation of plant pigments through paper chromatography
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Plant Physiology
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-11: Photosynthesis in Higher Plants

practicals.title: Study of the rate of respiration in flower buds/leaf tissue and germinating seeds
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Plant Physiology / Unit-V Human Physiology (Contextual)

practicals.title: Test for presence of urea, sugar, albumin and bile salts in urine
practicals.unit_name: Unit-V Human Physiology
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-16: Excretory Products and their Elimination

practicals.title: Study of pollen germination (temporary mount)
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VI Reproduction
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-1: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants

practicals.title: Study of plant population density and frequency by quadrat method
practicals.unit_name: Unit-X Ecology and Environment
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-12: Ecosystem / Chapter-13: Biodiversity and its Conservation

practicals.title: Preparation of temporary mount of onion root tip to study mitosis
practicals.unit_name: Unit-III Cell: Structure and Function / Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution (Contextual)

practicals.title: Isolation of DNA from plant material
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-5: Molecular Basis of Inheritance
practicals.details: Using spinach, green pea seeds, papaya, banana etc.

practicals.title: Study of flowers adapted to pollination by different agencies
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VI Reproduction / Unit-X Ecology and Environment (Contextual)
practicals.details: Observation for wind, insect, bird pollination.

practicals.title: Identification of stages of gamete development
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VI Reproduction / Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution (Contextual)
practicals.details: T.S. of testis and ovary through permanent slides.

practicals.title: Study of meiosis in onion bud cell or grasshopper testis
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution

practicals.title: Observation of T.S. of blastula (Mammalian)
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VI Reproduction / Unit-X Ecology and Environment (Contextual)

practicals.title: Study of Mendelian inheritance using seeds
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-4: Principles of Inheritance and Variation

practicals.title: Preparation of pedigree charts for genetic traits
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution / Unit-X Ecology and Environment (Contextual)
practicals.details: Traits: rolling tongue, blood groups, ear lobes, widow's peak, color blindness.

practicals.title: Controlled pollination - emasculation, tagging and bagging
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VI Reproduction / Unit-X Ecology and Environment (Contextual)

practicals.title: Identification of disease causing organisms
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VIII Biology and Human Welfare
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter-7: Human Health and Diseases / Chapter-8: Microbes in Human Welfare (Contextual)
practicals.details: Ascaris, Entamoeba, Plasmodium, fungus causing ringworm via slides/models.

practicals.title: Study of symbiotic associations
practicals.unit_name: Unit-X Ecology and Environment / Unit-VIII Biology and Human Welfare (Contextual)
practicals.details: Lichens, root nodules of leguminous plants, parasitic mode in Cuscuta.

practicals.title: Study of morphological adaptations to xerophytic conditions
practicals.unit_name: Unit-X Ecology and Environment / Unit-IV Plant Physiology (Contextual)

practicals.title: Identification of homologous and analogous organs
practicals.unit_name: Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution / Unit-X Ecology and Environment (Contextual)

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.unit_name: Unit-V Reproduction / Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution / Unit-X Ecology and Environment
projects.details: Conduct an investigatory project related to the curriculum topics. Maintain a project record including aim, apparatus, theory, procedure, skills, and precautions.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI (2026-27): Time 3 Hours, Max Marks 70.
Typology of questions includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions; SA; LA-I; LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions.
competency_weightage: Class XII (2026-27): Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding: 50%; Application of Knowledge / Concepts: 30%; Analyse, Evaluate and Create: 20%.
Internal choice of approximately 33% is provided.
bloom_levels: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize).
Application of Knowledge/Concepts (Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish).
Analyze, Evaluate and Create (Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct).
internal_assessment: Topics included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments: Digestion and Absorption (Class XI); Environmental Issues (Class XII).

Visually Impaired Students Practical Examination: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus: 5 marks; Written test (15 questions, answer any 10): 10 marks; Practical Records: 5 marks; Viva: 10 marks.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practicals include study of flowering plants from Solanaceae family, preparation and study of T.S. of dicot/monocot roots/stems, osmosis by potato osmometer, plasmolysis in epidermal peels, stomata distribution, transpiration rates, tests for sugar/starch/proteins/fats, paper chromatography, respiration rate studies, and urine analysis (urea, sugar, albumin, bile salts).
Class XII Practicals include pollen germination mount, plant population density/frequency by quadrat method, onion root tip mitosis study, DNA isolation from plant material.

Spotting components for Class XI: Microscope parts; specimens/slides/models (Bacteria to Rabbit); virtual specimens (Amoeba to Rabbit); Mitosis slides; inflorescence types; human skeleton models. Spotting components for Class XII: Flowers adapted to pollination, pollen germination slides, gamete development T.S., meiosis slides, blastula T.S., Mendelian inheritance seeds, pedigree charts, controlled pollination (emasculation/tagging/bagging), disease-causing organisms.

Visually Impaired Students Practical Examination includes identification of apparatus and materials such as compound microscope, beaker, test tubes, chromatography paper, soil samples, maize inflorescence, frog developmental models, Ascaris model, Cactus/Opuntia model.
project_assessment: Class XI: Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.

Class XII: Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks. Students are expected to record at least five of the listed experiments in their practical file, checked and signed by the internal examiner.

Interactions at organismal level up to ecosystems and biomes

subtopics: Five kingdom classification system
Salient features of Monera, Protista and Fungi
Classification into major groups: Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
key_concepts: Patterns of inheritance in terms of Mendel's laws at population level
Evidences of biological evolution and natural selection consequences

subtopics: Classification of plants into major groups: Algae, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnospermae and Angiosperms
key_concepts: Salient and distinguishing features with examples for each group
Changes in allele frequency structure and function due to evolution

subtopics: Classification of non-chordates up to phyla level and chordates upto class level
key_concepts: Salient features with examples for each category
Linkages between scientific knowledge across other curricular areas (e.g., sound pitch, muscle use in dance)

units.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

subtopics: Morphology of root, stem, leaf, inflorescence, flower, fruit and seed
key_concepts: Description of family Solanaceae

subtopics: Tissue systems in dicots and monocots
key_concepts: Anatomy and functions of tissue systems

subtopics: Morphology, Anatomy and functions of digestive system
key_concepts: Circulatory, respiratory, nervous and reproductive systems in frog

subtopics: Cell theory and cell as basic unit
key_concepts: Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells (Plant/Animal)
Cell envelope, membrane, wall
Organelles: ER, Golgi, Lysosomes, Vacuoles, Mitochondria, Ribosomes, Plastids, Microbodies
Cytoskeleton, cilia, flagella, centrioles (ultrastructure and function)
Nucleus

subtopics: Chemical constituents: Proteins, Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids
key_concepts: Structure and function of biomolecules
Enzyme types, properties and action

subtopics: Cell cycle phases
key_concepts: Mitosis and Meiosis processes
Significance of cell division

subtopics: Site of photosynthesis and pigments
key_concepts: Photochemical and biosynthetic phases
Cyclic and non-cyclic photophosphorylation
Chemiosmotic hypothesis, Photorespiration
C3 and C4 pathways
Factors affecting photosynthesis

subtopics: Exchange of gases and cellular respiration pathways
key_concepts: Glycolysis, Fermentation (anaerobic), TCA cycle, Electron transport system (aerobic)
Energy relations: ATP generation
Amphibolic pathways and Respiratory quotient

subtopics: Seed germination, phases of growth and rate
key_concepts: Conditions of growth: differentiation, dedifferentiation, redifferentiation
Sequence of developmental processes in plant cells
Plant growth regulators (Auxin, Gibberellin, Cytokinin, Ethylene, ABA)

subtopics: Flower structure and development of gametophytes
Pollination types, agencies, out-breeding devices, pollen-pistil interaction
Double fertilization and post-fertilization events (endosperm/embryo)
Development of seed and fruit formation
Special modes: apomixis, parthenocarpy, polyembryony
key_concepts: Significance of seed dispersal and fruit formation

subtopics: Male and female reproductive systems; microscopic anatomy of testis and ovary
Gametogenesis: spermatogenesis and oogenesis
Menstrual cycle, fertilization, embryo development to blastocyst
Implantation, pregnancy, placenta formation (elementary)
Parturition and lactation (elementary)
key_concepts: Elementary ideas of parturition and lactation

subtopics: Need for reproductive health and prevention of STDs
Birth control: need, methods, contraception
Medical Termination of Pregnancy (MTP) and amniocentesis
Infertility and Assisted Reproductive Technologies (IVF, ZIFT, GIFT)
General awareness on reproductive health issues
key_concepts: Assisted reproductive technologies for general awareness

subtopics: Mendelian inheritance and deviations (incomplete dominance, co-dominance)
Multiple alleles, blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance
Chromosome theory of inheritance; sex determination mechanisms
Linkage, crossing over, sex-linked inheritance (haemophilia, colour blindness)
Mendelian and chromosomal disorders in humans
key_concepts: Down's syndrome, Turner's, Klinefelter's syndromes; thalassemia

subtopics: Search for genetic material; DNA and RNA structure
DNA packaging, replication, Central Dogma (transcription/translation)
Genetic code and gene expression regulation (lac operon)
Human genome project and rice genome projects
DNA fingerprinting
key_concepts: Central dogma and genetic code application

subtopics: Origin of life and evidences for evolution (paleontology, anatomy, embryology)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory
Mechanism of evolution: variation via mutation and recombination

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.unit_name: Unit-V Reproduction / Unit-VII Genetics and Evolution / Unit-X Ecology and Environment
projects.details: Conduct an investigatory project related to the curriculum topics. Maintain a project record including aim, apparatus, theory, procedure, skills, and precautions.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI (2026-27): Time 3 Hours, Max Marks 70.
Typology of questions includes VSA including MCQs, Assertion–Reasoning type questions; SA; LA-I; LA-II; Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions.
competency_weightage: Class XII (2026-27): Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding: 50%; Application of Knowledge / Concepts: 30%; Analyse, Evaluate and Create: 20%.
Internal choice of approximately 33% is provided.
bloom_levels: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize).
Application of Knowledge/Concepts (Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish).
Analyze, Evaluate and Create (Interpret, analyse, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct).
internal_assessment: Topics included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments: Digestion and Absorption (Class XI); Environmental Issues (Class XII).

Visually Impaired Students Practical Examination: Time 2 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus: 5 marks; Written test (15 questions, answer any 10): 10 marks; Practical Records: 5 marks; Viva: 10 marks.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practicals include study of flowering plants from Solanaceae family, preparation and study of T.S. of dicot/monocot roots/stems, osmosis by potato osmometer, plasmolysis in epidermal peels, stomata distribution, transpiration rates, tests for sugar/starch/proteins/fats, paper chromatography, respiration rate studies, and urine analysis (urea, sugar, albumin, bile salts).
Class XII Practicals include pollen germination mount, plant population density/frequency by quadrat method, onion root tip mitosis study, DNA isolation from plant material.

Spotting components for Class XI: Microscope parts; specimens/slides/models (Bacteria to Rabbit); virtual specimens (Amoeba to Rabbit); Mitosis slides; inflorescence types; human skeleton models. Spotting components for Class XII: Flowers adapted to pollination, pollen germination slides, gamete development T.S., meiosis slides, blastula T.S., Mendelian inheritance seeds, pedigree charts, controlled pollination (emasculation/tagging/bagging), disease-causing organisms.

Visually Impaired Students Practical Examination includes identification of apparatus and materials such as compound microscope, beaker, test tubes, chromatography paper, soil samples, maize inflorescence, frog developmental models, Ascaris model, Cactus/Opuntia model.
project_assessment: Class XI: Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.

Class XII: Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks. Students are expected to record at least five of the listed experiments in their practical file, checked and signed by the internal examiner.

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competencies: C-3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

C-3.2 Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C-3.3 Describes mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competencies: C-4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic/ heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms
C-4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C-4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C-4.4 Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations)

C-4.5 Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competencies: C-5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history and the present times to the overall field of Science, including the disciplines that constitute it
competencies: C-6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge in order to appreciate that Science is ever evolving, and that there are still many unanswered questions
competencies: C-7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied — ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students
C-7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised to be inadequate

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competencies: C-8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results

C-8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)

Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.
